title: Drata Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Drata** is a continuous-compliance automation platform exposing a REST API suite under `https://public-api.drata.com`. This connector — `DrataConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "drata"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Drata workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Personnel | `/personnel` | List + read enrolled personnel |
| Controls | `/controls` | List + read controls across all active frameworks |
| Evidence | `/evidence` | List collected evidence, optionally filtered by control |
| Risks | `/risks` | Risk register entries |
| Vendors | `/vendors` | List + read tracked vendors |
| Audits | `/audits` | Audit instances (SOC 2 Type II, etc.) |
| Policies | `/policies` | Policy documents |
| Devices | `/devices` | Enrolled / monitored endpoints |
| Compliance Frameworks | `/frameworks` | Active frameworks (SOC2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, …) |

The connector normalises personnel + controls + evidence + risks + vendors into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::DrataHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Drata API key

1. Sign in to https://app.drata.com.
2. Open **Settings → API Keys**.
3. Click **Create API Key**, name it (e.g. `shielva-integration`), and grant the read scopes you need (Personnel Read, Controls Read, Evidence Read, etc.).
4. Copy the API key value — Drata shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Drata**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://public-api.drata.com` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `DrataHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists 1 personnel record as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Drata REST API uses **server-to-server Bearer API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <api_key>`.
- No additional account/workspace headers required — the API key is scoped to the issuing workspace.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Initialises `DrataHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Drata endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns an empty `TokenInfo` — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /personnel?limit=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://public-api.drata.com`) | Override for Drata sandbox or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DRATA_BASE = "https://public-api.drata.com"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `DrataConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::DrataHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; initialise `DrataHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo whose access_token is the configured api_key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 personnel record via `GET /personnel?limit=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Drata personnel + controls + evidence + risks + vendors → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Personnel
children.content:
### `async list_personnel(limit=100, offset=0, status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /personnel?limit={N}&offset={O}&status={S}` — list enrolled personnel.

### `async get_personnel(personnel_id) -> Dict`
`GET /personnel/{personnel_id}` — fetch a single personnel record.

children.title: Controls + Evidence
children.content:
### `async list_controls(limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /controls`.

### `async get_control(control_id) -> Dict`
`GET /controls/{control_id}`.

### `async list_evidence(limit=100, offset=0, control_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /evidence?controlId={control_id}` — list collected evidence, optionally filtered by control.

children.title: Risks + Vendors
children.content:
### `async list_risks(limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /risks` — risk register entries.

### `async list_vendors(limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /vendors` — tracked vendors.

### `async get_vendor(vendor_id) -> Dict`
`GET /vendors/{vendor_id}`.

children.title: Audits · Policies · Devices · Frameworks
children.content:
### `async list_audits(limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /audits` — audit instances.

### `async list_policies(limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /policies` — policy documents.

### `async list_devices(limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /devices` — enrolled / monitored endpoints.

### `async list_frameworks() -> Dict`
`GET /frameworks` — active compliance frameworks.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
DrataError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── DrataAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── DrataBadRequestError                # 400
├── DrataNotFoundError                  # 404
├── DrataConflictError                  # 409
├── DrataRateLimitError                 # 429 — retry_after_s
└── DrataServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
DrataNetworkError = DrataServerError    # legacy name from older code
DrataNotFound     = DrataNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `DrataNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the key in ACP. Drata shows the key only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Drata — API key lacks scope | In Drata Dashboard → API Keys, edit the key and grant Personnel/Controls/Evidence/Risks read scopes. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — API key invalid or revoked | Verify the key matches the workspace. Rotate the key if needed. |
| `drata.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Drata per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_personnel` / `get_control` / `get_vendor` | Wrong ID — does not exist or belongs to another workspace | Re-list the parent collection and use a returned `id` value. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'DrataNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `DrataNetworkError = DrataServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
